text:
Debugging is an essential skill for any programmer. The first step is identifying bugs, which often involves noticing unexpected behavior in your code. A systematic approach is key: instead of guessing, work through your code step by step. For example, you can check the most recent changes or test small sections individually.

Using debugging tools can speed up this process. Most development environments include built-in debuggers that let you pause the program and inspect variables. Another simple but powerful tool is logging—adding print statements or log entries to track what your code does at different points. By comparing expected and actual output, you can locate the exact problem, fix it, and test again.
